objective: Make stale context detection routine by adding a lightweight agent-readiness context-health projection to the existing agent-readiness deep module, with tests that classify missing or stale Project Brain, active artifact, runtime-card, and external-horizon context before agents act on old evidence.
feedbackSignal: Goal continuation after confirming audit freshness linkage; next progress must close another adopted evidence-led gap instead of stopping at audit-link validation.
rootOperationalFailure: Context freshness is still distributed across individual docs, active-artifact routing, runtime cards, and memory surfaces; agents can orient from stale context unless a routine command reports context-health explicitly.
durableSystemImprovement: agent-readiness emits agent-readiness-context-health/v1 with machine-readable surface statuses, evidence-use classification, stale reasons, and suggested refresh commands, while preserving the existing context-health command as the heavier context-integrity artifact generator.

inScope: Add agent-readiness-context-health/v1 as a summary field on agent-readiness/v1 reports.

Classify Project Brain active-artifacts presence, active-route references, Project Brain memory/knowledge presence, runtime-card recency absence, and unobserved external horizon as context-health surfaces.
Emit suggested refresh commands that are advisory and do not mutate repository state.
Reference the existing harness context-health command as a deeper refresh path instead of duplicating its context-integrity report engine.
Keep the projection advisory and non-blocking by default; do not add a strict-context mode in this slice.
Add focused tests for passing context health, missing referenced active-route files, missing Project Brain memory/knowledge, and JSON CLI visibility.
outOfScope: Fetching live GitHub, CI, Linear, or CodeRabbit state.
Treating stale context orientation evidence as delivery claim support.

assumptions: agent-readiness is the narrowest existing cockpit command for routine local context-health without widening harness next.
The existing context-health command remains the canonical context-integrity artifact/report lane; this slice only adds fast readiness metadata and suggestions.
The first patch can use local filesystem and current repo evidence only; network-backed PR/CI/Linear freshness remains a later external-state slice.
Warnings are acceptable for absent optional runtime-card or external-horizon context, while missing mandatory Project Brain route evidence can fail.
acceptanceCriteria: agent-readiness JSON includes a contextHealth object with schemaVersion agent-readiness-context-health/v1.
contextHealth surfaces include active_artifacts, active_route_refs, project_brain_memory, project_brain_knowledge, runtime_card, and external_horizon.
contextHealth suggested refresh commands include the existing context-health command for deeper context-integrity evidence.
Missing active-artifacts route references produce a context_health finding with concrete missing paths and a refresh/remediation recommendation.
Missing Project Brain memory or knowledge surfaces are classified before closeout rather than hidden in prose.
Runtime-card and external-horizon absence are marked orientation-only warnings, not delivery claim support.
Focused tests prove the report shape and at least one stale/missing context failure path.
Focused tests prove default context-health projection findings are warnings unless they reflect an existing required readiness failure outside the projection.
Focused tests prove agent-readiness does not emit context-health-report/v1 fields such as artifactRefs, coverage metrics, contradiction history, or inventory metrics.

Focused tests prove suggested context-health refresh guidance is prerequisite-aware: recommend the deeper context-health report when harness.contract.json has contextIntegrityPolicy, otherwise recommend bootstrap/contract remediation before the deep command.
Goal-board validation still passes after the slice receipt is recorded.

projectionContract.precedenceRule: agent-readiness-context-health/v1 is advisory readiness metadata for orientation only. context-health-report/v1 remains the canonical artifact-grade context-integrity report.
projectionContract.antiDuplicationRule: agent-readiness must not duplicate contradiction history, coverage-rate, inventory, memory-metrics snapshot, or context-integrity artifact generation logic from src/commands/context-health.ts.
projectionContract.defaultExitPolicy: The context-health projection must not introduce new exit-1 behavior in default agent-readiness runs. Missing or stale projection surfaces are warn unless the same condition already fails an existing required readiness check.

suggestionPrerequisites.contextHealthReportCommand: Suggest node --import tsx src/cli.ts context-health --json only when harness.contract.json exists and declares contextIntegrityPolicy.
suggestionPrerequisites.fallbackCommand: When context-health prerequisites are absent, suggest node --import tsx src/cli.ts --help --all-commands or node --import tsx src/cli.ts init --dry-run --json before the report command.

surfaceSeverityMatrix.condition: .harness/active-artifacts.md exists and has Current Active Route plus Artifact Index
.harness/active-artifacts.md is missing or lacks required headings
Current Active Route references repo paths and every referenced path exists
Current Active Route references missing repo paths
No local runtime-card artifact is discoverable for the current slice
No live PR/CI/Linear/CodeRabbit snapshot is provided to agent-readiness

validationGates: git diff --check -- src/lib/agent-readiness src/commands/agent-readiness.test.ts docs/cli-reference.md README.md .harness/implementation-notes/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit.mdx docs/goals/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit
rollback: Remove contextHealth report fields and context_health findings from agent-readiness.
Remove PU-019 tests and receipt lines if the intent review blocks the slice before implementation.
Keep existing agent-readiness categories unchanged if context-health introduces unexpected compatibility risk.
stopConditions: Intent reviewers find the slice should live only in the existing context-health command or in harness next instead of agent-readiness.
Existing agent-readiness compatibility tests fail in a way that requires changing the public command contract beyond additive metadata.
